info.title: United Logistics API
info.description: Industry-grade public API for fleet, fuel, and toll operations. Every endpoint is scoped to the organization owning the API key. Authentication: bearer API key (`ulk_<prefix>_<secret>`). Rate limit: 60 requests/minute/key by default (override per key via the dashboard).

ApiKeyAuth.description: Authorization: Bearer ulk_<prefix>_<secret>. Cree la clave en Configuración → API.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Null when there are no more results.

Provider.description: Registered: karma (Karma sync), internal (created in the app). Custom integrators use a stable lowercase slug.

vehicle_count.description: Number of vehicles assigned to this operation (when requested).

time_zone.description: IANA time zone for trip and event timestamps (e.g. America/Santo_Domingo).

type.description: Raw vehicle type string as reported by the provider (e.g. 'truck', 'sedan').

provider_metadata.description: Free-form provider-specific structured metadata. Schema varies by provider.

vehicle_type.description: User-curated vehicle classification used for analytics (overrides `type`).

driver_id.description: Provider driver external_id at time of snapshot (`drivers.external_id`).
Provider driver external_id currently operating this vehicle (`drivers.external_id`). Optional — pass `null` to clear.

external_id.description: Org-scoped integer id used as the natural key when paired with `provider`.

driver_key.description: Unique RFID/credential the driver presents on the in-vehicle device.

vehicle_external_id.description: The provider's id for the vehicle that emitted this event. Pair with /api/v1/vehicles?include_inactive=true to resolve.

fleet_id.description: Fleet (provider account) scope. Required on each event; used for access validation.

provider.description: Telematics provider slug (e.g. `karma`, `queclink`, custom integrator slug).

external_message_id.description: Provider message id. Required for idempotent bulk inserts; unique per `fleet_id`.

event_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event occurred.

extra.description: Opaque provider payload (driver key, raw extras, etc.).

fleet_id.description: Fleet (provider account) scope. Required on each event; used for access validation.

provider.description: Telematics provider slug (e.g. `karma`, `queclink`, custom integrator slug).

external_message_id.description: Provider message id. Required for idempotent bulk inserts; unique per `fleet_id`.

event_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event occurred.

extra.description: Opaque provider payload (driver key, raw extras, etc.).

ids.description: Numeric event ids to delete. Scoped to the API key's organization.

id.description: Internal numeric id. Stable per org.

vehicle_number.description: Denormalized vehicle number at the time of the trip.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trip started.

ids.description: Internal ids of every imported (inserted or updated) trip.

vehicle_number.description: Denormalized vehicle number at the time of the trip.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trip started.

vehicle_number.description: Denormalized vehicle number at the time of the trip.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trip started.

green_max.description: Upper bound (kph) for the 'green' speed bucket.

yellow_max.description: Upper bound (kph) for the 'yellow' speed bucket.

red_threshold.description: Lower bound (kph) where the 'red' (over-speed) bucket begins.

idle_max_percentage.description: Maximum allowed idle-time percentage before the vehicle is flagged.

external_id.description: Provider fleet id (legacy Karma project id).

id.description: Internal numeric POI id. Stable for the lifetime of the POI.

fleet_id.description: Fleet (provider account) this POI is scoped to.

geometry_type.description: `circle` for radius-based geofences, `polygon` for arbitrary shapes.

geofence_radius_meters.description: Radius in meters for circle geofences (10-1000). `null` for polygons.

polygon_coordinates.description: Polygon vertices as `[latitude, longitude]` tuples. Closed automatically (no need to repeat the first point).

tags.description: User-defined tags used for filtering and reports.

fleet_id.description: Fleet (provider account) this POI is scoped to.

description.example: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

image_url.description: Public image URL (typically Supabase storage `poi-images` bucket).

geometry_type.description: `circle` for radius-based geofences, `polygon` for arbitrary shapes.

latitude.description: Required for `circle`. For `polygon`, derived from centroid if omitted.

longitude.description: Required for `circle`. For `polygon`, derived from centroid if omitted.

polygon_coordinates.description: Required for `polygon`. At least 3 `[lat, lng]` vertices; the polygon is closed automatically.

tags.description: User-defined tags for filtering and reports.

image_url.description: Optional photo of the toll booth (legacy `image` column).

vehicle_external_id.description: Vehicle currently attributed to this charge (post-matching). Legacy `vehicle_id`.

matched_vehicle_external_id.description: Vehicle the validation pipeline matched against (legacy `matched_vehicle_id`).

vehicle_external_id.description: External id of the GPS vehicle (legacy `vehicle_id`). Null for non-GPS tags.

has_gps.description: Whether the assigned vehicle is GPS-tracked. Defaults to true.

vehicle_external_id.description: External id of the GPS vehicle (maps to `vehicle_id`). Required when `has_gps` is true.

vehicle_title.description: Free-text label for a non-GPS vehicle. Required when `has_gps` is false.

vehicle_license_plate.description: License plate for a non-GPS vehicle. Required when `has_gps` is false.

vehicle_external_id.description: Provider vehicle id (legacy `vehicle_id`). Enables trip/GPS matching.

vehicle_number.description: Denormalized vehicle number/plate at the time of the refuel.

total_cost.description: Transaction total; price_per_gallon is inferred when provided.

source.description: Origin of the entry. `manual` for UI capture, `import` for bulk CSV/Excel, `API` for integrations. Legacy `ERM` is accepted and stored as `manual`.

vehicle_external_id.description: Assign or clear the linked vehicle (legacy `vehicle_id`).

source.description: Origin of the entry. `manual` for UI capture, `import` for bulk CSV/Excel, `API` for integrations. Legacy `ERM` is accepted and stored as `manual`.

route_id.description: Logical route id (groups versions of the same route).

get.summary: Get the organization that owns the API key
get.description: Returns the organization tied to the bearer API key plus its enabled modules and a couple of headline counts. Use this on integration boot-up to confirm the key is healthy and the org has the modules you expect.
Returns operations owned by your organization. Operations are the canonical way to group vehicles per org (replacing cross-org project assignment).

post.description: Creates a new operation for your organization.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a operation. Fails if vehicles are still assigned or if this is the only default operation.

post.description: Creates a vehicle for your organization. `external_id` must be unique per org. `time_zone` must be an allowed IANA identifier.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of vehicles belonging to the API key's organization. Ordered by `external_id` ascending so the cursor is stable. Active (non-deactivated) vehicles only unless `include_inactive=true`.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.
Set to `true` to include deactivated vehicles. Defaults to `false`.
Filter by GPS provider slug (`karma`, `internal`, or your custom slug). Recommended when syncing a single feed.
Filter by provider-scoped external_id (integer). Use with `provider` for natural-key lookup.

get.description: Returns a single vehicle by its internal UUID `id`. To look up by Karma `external_id`, use the list endpoint with a filter.

patch.description: Partially updates a vehicle. Set `reactivate: true` to clear `deactivated_at` and restore an inactive vehicle.

delete.description: Soft-deactivates a vehicle by setting `deactivated_at`. The row remains in the database and can be restored with `PATCH` + `reactivate: true`.

patch.description: Updates the stored location snapshot for one vehicle. Identify the vehicle by `id` (UUID) or by `external_id` + `provider`. Optional `driver_id` (provider driver external id) may be included when the engine is on. Does not modify unrelated vehicle fields.

get.description: Returns stored location snapshots for the org (same field names as the legacy Karma location payload). Used by the live map and integrator diagnostics.

schema.description: Comma-separated external_id values. Omit to return all vehicles with a stored location.
Optional filter to one provider slug.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of drivers for the API key's organization. Drivers are sorted by `external_id` ascending so the cursor is stable.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.
Set to `true` to include drivers with `deactivated_at` set. Defaults to `false`.
Filter by GPS provider slug (`karma`, `internal`, or your custom slug).
Filter by provider-scoped external_id (integer). Use with `provider` for natural-key lookup.

post.description: Creates a driver for your organization. `(provider, external_id)` must be unique per org. `driver_key` (RFID) must also be unique per org.

get.description: Returns a single driver by its internal UUID.

patch.description: Partially updates a driver. Set `reactivate: true` to clear `deactivated_at` and restore an inactive driver.

delete.description: Soft-deactivates a driver by setting `deactivated_at`. Pass `?permanent=true` to hard-delete an already-inactive driver.

schema.description: Pass `true` to hard-delete (only allowed for inactive drivers).

get.description: Returns telemetry events scoped to the API key's org. Cursor-paginated on (event_date desc, id desc). For dense historical pulls, prefer narrow time ranges to keep latency low.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call.
Filter to events emitted by a specific vehicle (provider external id).
ISO timestamp. Only events with `event_date >= start` are returned.
ISO timestamp. Only events with `event_date <= end` are returned.

error.message: limit must be an integer between 1 and 500.

post.description: Inserts one event object or an array of up to 1000 events. Idempotent on `(external_message_id, fleet_id)` — duplicate rows are skipped. `fleet_id` and `external_message_id` are required on each event.

error.message: limit must be an integer between 1 and 500.
API key lacks the required scope.

delete.description: Permanently deletes up to 1000 events by numeric `id`. Only events belonging to the API key's organization are removed; unknown ids are counted in `not_found` (no error).

error.message: limit must be an integer between 1 and 500.
API key lacks the required scope.

get.description: Returns a single telemetry event by its numeric `id` (scoped to the API key's organization).

error.message: limit must be an integer between 1 and 500.
limit must be an integer between 1 and 500.
API key lacks the required scope.

get.description: Returns trips scoped to the API key's org. Cursor-paginated on `(start_date desc, id desc)`. Filter by vehicle and/or `start_date` range.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.
Return trips whose `start_date` is >= this ISO 8601 timestamp.
Return trips whose `start_date` is <= this ISO 8601 timestamp.

post.description: Creates a trip for your organization. The vehicle (`vehicle_external_id`) must belong to the API key's org. `external_id` must be unique per org.

error.message: No tienes permisos para modificar viajes.

404.description: Vehicle not found in this organization

error.message: El vehículo especificado no existe en esta organización.

409.description: Trip with this external_id already exists

error.message: Ya existe un viaje con ese external_id para esta organización.

post.description: Imports a single trip or an array of up to 1000 trips for your organization. Idempotent on `(external_id, org_id)`: trips that already exist are updated, the rest are inserted — so you can safely re-send the same batch. Every `vehicle_external_id` must belong to the API key's org. Duplicate `external_id`s within one request are collapsed (last one wins).

error.message: No tienes permisos para modificar viajes.

404.description: A referenced vehicle does not exist in this organization

error.message: El vehículo especificado no existe en esta organización.

get.description: Returns a single trip by its numeric `id` (scoped to the API key's organization).

patch.description: Partially updates a trip. Send only the fields you want to change. `external_id`, `vehicle_external_id`, `provider` and `org_id` cannot be changed once set.

error.message: No tienes permisos para modificar viajes.
No tienes permisos para modificar viajes.

get.description: **Deprecated** — use `GET /fleets` for org fleet groupings. This endpoint lists legacy GPS-provider sync fleets (Karma) still used by the internal sync pipeline.

get.summary: List vehicle brands and velocity thresholds
get.description: Returns vehicle brands (legacy `marcas`) for your org. Each brand carries the velocity buckets and fuel-efficiency bounds applied to vehicles assigned to it.
Returns Karma GPS fleets (sync scopes) your organization owns or has been granted access to.

get.summary: Get a GPS fleet by id

get.description: Returns POIs (geofences) for your org. Each POI is either a circle (point + `geofence_radius_meters`) or a polygon (`polygon_coordinates`). Ordered by `id` ascending so the cursor is stable.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.

data.name: Cliente Altice – Bávaro
data.description: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

post.description: Creates a circle (default) or polygon POI for your organization. The fleet must be owned by or assigned to your org. For circles, `latitude`, `longitude` and `geofence_radius_meters` (10-1000) are required. For polygons, `polygon_coordinates` with at least 3 `[lat, lng]` vertices is required; `latitude`/`longitude` will be set to the polygon centroid automatically.

value.name: Cliente Altice – Bávaro
value.description: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

data.name: Cliente Altice – Bávaro
data.description: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

geofence_radius_meters: geofence_radius_meters es requerido para geocercas circulares

error.message: La flota no está asignada a tu organización.

get.description: Returns a single POI by its numeric `id` (scoped to the API key's organization).

data.name: Cliente Altice – Bávaro
data.description: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

geofence_radius_meters: geofence_radius_meters es requerido para geocercas circulares

patch.description: Partially updates a POI. Send only the fields you want to change. To switch geometry, include `geometry_type` plus either `latitude`/`longitude`/`geofence_radius_meters` (for `circle`) or `polygon_coordinates` (for `polygon`).

switch_to_polygon.summary: Switch geometry from circle to polygon

data.name: Cliente Altice – Bávaro (Principal)
data.description: Punto de entrega cliente Altice zona este

geofence_radius_meters: geofence_radius_meters es requerido para geocercas circulares

error.message: La flota no está asignada a tu organización.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a POI. Returns `409 conflict` if the POI is referenced by any scheduled route stops — remove the route stops first.

geofence_radius_meters: geofence_radius_meters es requerido para geocercas circulares

error.message: La flota no está asignada a tu organización.

409.description: POI is referenced by a scheduled route

error.message: Este POI no se puede eliminar porque está referenciado por rutas programadas. Elimina las paradas primero.

get.description: Returns POI visits (geofence entries/exits) for your org, cursor-paginated on (`visit_start desc`, `id desc`). A visit is one entry-to-exit cycle: a vehicle entered the geofence, stayed for some time, then left. The fields prefixed `poi_*` and `vehicle_*` are denormalized snapshots so historical visits remain stable even when the underlying POI/vehicle is later edited.

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.

get.description: Returns the global Paso Rápido toll catalog with the price for each of the 5 vehicle categories.
Returns Paso Rápido toll charges for your org, ordered by date desc. Includes the validation-pipeline outputs (`validation_status`, `fraud_type`, `location_validation_status`, etc.).
Returns Paso Rápido tag assignments for your org, ordered by `id` desc. Supports filtering by `tag_number`, `status` and `has_gps`.

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.

post.description: Creates a tag assignment. When `has_gps` is true, `vehicle_external_id` is required; otherwise `vehicle_title` and `vehicle_license_plate` are required. `tag_number` is unique per organization.

get.summary: Get a tag assignment by id
get.description: Returns a single tag assignment by its numeric `id`.

patch.description: Partially updates a tag assignment. Changing `has_gps` or any vehicle field re-normalizes the GPS/manual vehicle fields together.

get.description: Returns fuel entries (refills, fleet card transactions, ad-hoc imports) for your org, cursor-paginated on `(transaction_date desc, id desc)`. Filter by vehicle and/or `transaction_date` range.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call's `pagination.next_cursor`.
Return entries whose `transaction_date` is >= this ISO 8601 timestamp.
Return entries whose `transaction_date` is <= this ISO 8601 timestamp.

post.description: Creates a fuel entry for your organization. When `vehicle_external_id` and `transaction_date` are provided, the entry is automatically matched against the nearest trip and GPS message (±30 min). `odometer_reading` drives the efficiency trigger.

error.message: API key is missing required scope: fuel_entries:write.

404.description: Vehicle not found in this organization

error.message: El vehículo especificado no existe en esta organización.

get.summary: Get a fuel entry by id
get.description: Returns a single fuel entry by its UUID (scoped to the API key's org).

patch.description: Partially updates a fuel entry. Send only the fields you want to change. `org_id` cannot be changed.

error.message: API key is missing required scope: fuel_entries:write.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a fuel entry (historical record).

error.message: API key is missing required scope: fuel_entries:write.

post.description: Creates a single entry or an array of up to 1000 fuel entries for your organization. Every `vehicle_external_id` (when provided) must belong to the API key's org. Telemetry (trip, GPS message, odometer + position) is auto-matched best-effort for entries with a vehicle + `transaction_date`; pass `trip_external_id` / `matched_message_id` to skip matching for that entry.

error.message: API key is missing required scope: fuel_entries:write.

404.description: A referenced vehicle does not exist in this organization

error.message: El vehículo especificado no existe en esta organización.

get.description: Returns fuel products configured for your org (diesel, regular, premium, etc.).
Returns fuel vendors / gas stations configured for your org.
Returns scheduled routes (legacy `scheduled_routes`) for your org, ordered by `scheduled_start_time` desc.
Returns tokenized public share links generated for trips / map snapshots. The bearer of the link can view the trip without an account; revoke by setting `expires_at` in the dashboard.
